samples.xml_description: Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal traffic light operation, the east-west (EW) cycle is 60 clock cycles of green, 5 clock cycles of yellow, 10 clock cycles of red, and the north-south (NS) cycle is 10 clock cycles of green, 5 clock cycles of yellow, 60 clock cycles of red. The pedestrian button, when pressed, reduces the remaining EW green time to 10 clocks if the remaining time is greater than 10 clocks, and has no effect if the remaining time is less than 10 clocks. The EW and NS cycles are not the same length, so the counter should be 16 bits, with a maximum value of 63. Reset signal (active low). Clock signal. Pedestrian button input. Red output signal. Yellow output signal. Green output signal. An 8-bit output representing the count value of the internal counter. Idle state. State 1 - Red signal. State 2 - Yellow signal. State 3 - Green signal. State transition logic. <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <always> <description>The first always block is responsible for the state transition logic.</description> <for> <description>The case statement handles state transitions based on the current state.</description> <switch>$state</switch> <case> <description>Idle state where all signals are set to 0.</description> <condition>$state == $idle</condition> <action> <description>Set all signals to 0.</description> $red = 0; $yellow = 0; $green = 0; $next_state = $s1_red; </action> </case> <case> <description>Set the red signal to 1 and wait for a count of 3 before transitioning to s3_green state.</description> <condition>$state == $s1_red</condition> <action> $red = 1; $yellow = 0; $green = 0; if ($cnt == 3) begin $next_state = $s3_green; end else begin $next_state = $s1_red; end </action> </case> <case> <description>Set the yellow signal to 1 and wait for a count of 3 before transitioning to s1_red state.</description> <condition>$state == $s2_yellow</condition> <action> $red = 0; $yellow = 1; $green = 0; if ($cnt == 3) begin $next_state = $s1_red; end else begin $next_state = $s2_yellow; end </action> </case> <case> <description>Set the green signal to 1 and wait for a count of 3 before transitioning to s2_yellow state.</description> <condition>$state == $s3_green</condition> <action> $red = 0; $yellow = 0; $green = 1; if ($cnt == 3) begin $next_state = $s2_yellow; end else begin $next_state = $s3_green; end </action> </case> </for> </always> Reset and pass logic. <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <always> <description>The second always block handles the reset and pass logic.</description> <if> <description>If reset signal is active, set counter to 10.</description> if (!$rst_n) begin $cnt = 10; end else if ($pass_request && $green) begin <description>If pass request signal is active and green signal is active, set counter to 10.</description> $cnt = 10; end else if (!$green && $p_green) begin <description>If green signal is inactive and previous green signal is active, set counter to 60.</description> $cnt = 60; end else if (!$yellow && $p_yellow) begin <description>If yellow signal is inactive and previous yellow signal is active, set counter to 5.</description> $cnt = 5; end else if (!$red && $p_red) begin <description>If red signal is inactive and previous red signal is active, set counter to 10.</description> $cnt = 10; end else begin $cnt = $cnt - 1; end </if> </always> Output assignment. <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <always> <description>The final always block handles the output assignment.</description> <for> <description>Assign the previous values of the internal signals to the outputs.</description> <switch>$state</switch> <case> <description>Idle state where all outputs are 0.</description> <condition>$state == $idle</condition> <action> $red = 0; $yellow = 0; $green = 0; </action> </case> <case> <description>Set red output to 1.</description> <condition>$state == $s1_red</condition> <action> $red = 1; $yellow = 0; $green = 0; </action> </case> <case> <description>Set yellow output to 1.</description> <condition>$state == $s2_yellow</condition> <action> $red = 0; $yellow = 1; $green = 0; </action> </case> <case> <description>Set green output to 1.</description> <condition>$state == $s3_green</condition> <action> $red = 0; $yellow = 0; $green = 1; </action> </case> </for> </always> The first always block is responsible for the state transition logic. It uses a case statement to handle different states. Here's a summary of each state: - **idle**: Initial state where all signals are set to 0. Transition to s1_red state occurs immediately. - **s1_red**: Sets the red signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s3_green state. Otherwise, it remains in s1_red state. - **s2_yellow**: Sets the yellow signal to 1 and waits for a count of 3 before transitioning to s1_red state. Otherwise, it remains in s2_yellow state.
